disposal_reference_number.definition: The unique number assigned to the disposal of an object or group of objects. The number should also refer to a file containing all written documentation about the disposal.
disposal_reference_number.how_to_record: May include: a prefix to distinguish it from other reference numbers; the year; and a running number.
disposal_reference_number.use: The same Disposal reference number can be used for one or more objects disposed of together, but an object can only have one Disposal reference number.

disposal_reason.definition: The reason or justification for an object or objects being disposed of.
examples: This engine is no longer in working, there is a duplicate in good condition already in the collection.
disposal_reason.use: Record once only for an object.

disposal_authoriser.definition: The Person giving final approval for a disposal to take place.
disposal_authoriser.how_to_record: It will be necessary to use several units of information, including for example, a surname and a forename. The descriptions for these information units are gathered together under the Person heading. The organisation may have standard forms of names for use.
disposal_authoriser.use: Record once only for a disposal.

disposal_authorisation_date.definition: The date on which the Disposal authoriser gives final approval for a disposal to proceed.
disposal_authorisation_date.how_to_record: Always record the day, month and year in the same order. Always use the same character to separate each component. Be as specific as possible and indicate if the date is not known. Always give all four digits for the year.
disposal_authorisation_date.use: Record once only for a disposal.

disposal_method.definition: The way in which an object is disposed of.
disposal_method.how_to_record: Use a single term, without punctuation or capitalisation. Maintain a list of standard terms.
disposal_method.use: Record once only for an object or group of objects.

deaccession_date.definition: The date when an item was struck off the accession register.
deaccession_date.how_to_record: Always record the day, month and year in the same order. Always use the same character to separate each component. Be as specific as possible and indicate if the date is not known. Record all four digits for the year.
deaccession_date.use: Record once only for a disposal.

disposal_date.definition: The date an object is disposed of.
disposal_date.how_to_record: Always record the day, month and year in the same order. Always use the same character to separate each component. Be as specific as possible and indicate if the date is not known. Always give all four digits for the year.
disposal_date.use: Record once only for a disposal.

disposal_new_object_number.definition: The object number assigned to a deaccessioned object by the recipient.
disposal_new_object_number.use: Record once only for an object disposed of to another organisation.

disposal_note.definition: Additional information about the disposal of an object which has not been recorded elsewhere using controlled terminology.
examples: Infestation spreading, object isolated but needs to be disposed of as soon as possible.
disposal_note.use: Record once only for an object

disposal_price.definition: The total price paid for an object when disposed of through sale. Associated with the Object number. If no individual prices are known for specific objects in the group, use Group disposal price.
disposal_price.how_to_record: Use local currency. Convert any non-local currency into local, using the exchange rate at date of disposal. Use Disposal note to record details of non-local currency paid.
disposal_price.use: An object can only have one Object disposal price recorded.

disposal_provisos.definition: Restrictions applying to all the objects to be disposed of.
examples: Disposal recipient to be within the county.
disposal_provisos.use: Record once only for a disposal

disposal_recipient.definition: The People, Person or Organisation receiving an object to be disposed of by way of transfer, gift or sale.
disposal_recipient.how_to_record: It will be necessary to use several units of information, including for example, for a Person, a surname and a forename. The descriptions for these information units are gathered together under the People, Person or Organisation headings as appropriate. The organisation may have standard forms of names for use.
examples: See under People, Person or Organisation.
disposal_recipient.use: Record once only for a disposal.

group_disposal_price.definition: The total price paid for a group of objects at disposal including taxes for which the purchase is liable. If individual prices are known for specific objects in the group, use Disposal price.
group_disposal_price.how_to_record: Use local currency. Convert any non-local currency into local, using the exchange rate at date of disposal. Use Disposal note to record details of non-local currency paid.
group_disposal_price.use: A Group disposal price can be used for a number of objects, but any object can only have a single Group disposal price.
